heading: Grading of tobacco
label: 511s Grading of tobacco

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary of Agriculture (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”) shall conduct referenda among producers of each kind of tobacco that is eligible for price support under the Agricultural Act of 1949 ( et seq.) to determine whether such producers favor the mandatory grading of that kind of tobacco by the Secretary.March 31, 20027 U.S.C. 1421

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that mandatory grading is favored by a majority of the producers of a kind of tobacco voting in the referendum, the Secretary is authorized and directed to ensure that the kind of tobacco is graded at the time of sale effective for the 2002 and subsequent marketing years.

children.heading: Fees
children.label: B Fees
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall establish, collect, and use fees for the grading of tobacco required under this section in the same manner as user fees for the grading of tobacco sold at auction authorized under the Tobacco Inspection Act ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 511

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: 3 Judicial review
children.text: A determination by the Secretary under this section shall not be subject to judicial review.
